does: Bloom is an open-source automated pipeline that generates customizable evaluation suites to measure behavioral traits in large language models without needing ground-truth labels. The system constructs evaluation scenarios tailored to specific behaviors of interest, running these tests against frontier LLMs to produce measurements of how models exhibit particular traits. The approach automates the creation and administration of behavioral tests, enabling researchers to define behavioral dimensions they care about and receive quantitative assessments of how well models instantiate those behaviors, all without relying on costly human-annotated ground truth.
connects: This work builds on the growing challenge of evaluating LLMs beyond traditional benchmarks, extending prior efforts to measure model behaviors like toxicity, truthfulness, and alignment. It responds to limitations in existing evaluation frameworks that either require human labeling at scale or measure narrow predefined capabilities. The pipeline draws on techniques for automated test generation and configurable evaluation design, adapting these ideas to the behavioral assessment problem where ground truth is inherently ambiguous. It connects to parallel work in interpretability and probing that seeks to characterize model properties without explicit supervision.
enables: Bloom opens pathways for rapid behavioral characterization of new models and continuous monitoring of model properties as they evolve. The automated nature allows researchers to explore novel behavioral dimensions and iterate on evaluation designs quickly. This infrastructure could enable systematic comparison of behavioral profiles across model families, support red-teaming and safety analysis by generating targeted behavioral tests, and facilitate research into how architectural or training choices affect emergent behaviors. The tool potentially accelerates the development of behavioral baselines and could inform standards for responsible deployment by making behavioral auditing more accessible and scalable.
